ops.title: SocketServer: v2 sessions, serial accept queue, bind/shutdown order

ops.body:
# Serial accept queue (the GCD trap — unchanged in v2)

The accept loop runs on a **serial** DispatchQueue (`acceptQueue`, label-only construction = serial), never a concurrent one: a blocking `accept(2)` on a concurrent queue makes GCD spawn a fresh worker thread per pending block, exploding the thread pool. The serial queue holds exactly one blocked accept; accepted descriptors are handed off to a separate session queue.

# v2 session discipline (7623022)

Persistent multiplexed exact-v2 sessions over the SessionTransport.swift codec (length-prefixed frames, fail-closed SessionTransportError — the twin of wire/frame.go). `trust.check(descriptor:)` (PeerTrust) runs BEFORE the handshake and any frame processing; a failing peer's descriptor closes with no session and no reply. The mandatory handshake is bounded by SO_RCVTIMEO = `handshakeTimeout` on the descriptor; an empty build is refused. Request streams are bounded by `streamQueueDepth`; server events push through the session's serialized writer (`SocketSession.pushEvent`).

# Bind and shutdown (shape unchanged)

start(): if the path exists, a live peer answering a connect ping refuses the bind (addressInUse); only a dead socket (connect fails) is unlinked and rebound; the socket is chmod 0600 before accepting. stop(): stops intake, disconnects sessions, joins their request tasks (`acceptQueue.sync {}` joins the accept loop), then unlinks the path. Safe to call more than once.

# Historical (v1, replaced in 7623022)

One request line in, one reply line out per connection, \n-framed and capped at maxLineBytes; per-connection SO_RCVTIMEO bounded socket READS but never the handler, so shutdown could block behind a stuck handler. Replaced wholesale by the framed session protocol above.
